new_bullets: when asked "Find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S2016972. If the study was performed more than 12 months ago, order a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast indication 'Renal mass follow-up'", you must actually execute the FHIR query to retrieve the date of the most recent CT Abdomen, then compare that date to the current date, and only order a new study if a prior procedure exists and its date is more than 12 months old — do not simply return the query string without executing it and performing the comparison and ordering steps.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order has a matching naloxone prescription, you must first retrieve all active opioid orders by searching for opioid ingredients (e.g., morphine, hydromorphone, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone) using MedicationRequest with status "active" and the correct patient identifier, and for each opioid order found that lacks a matching active naloxone prescription, create a naloxone order — do not stop after merely checking for the existence of any naloxone order or after finding only one opioid order without creating the missing naloxone prescription.

when asked "Verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S3241217 has a matching naloxone prescription", you must first perform a thorough FHIR search for all active opioid orders by querying MedicationRequest with status "active" and the patient identifier, specifically searching for opioid ingredients such as hydromorphone, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone, and morphine — do not conclude that no opioid orders exist based on a general medication list that may exclude or miss opioid analgesics, and for each opioid order found, create a naloxone order if no matching active naloxone prescription exists.

when asked "Determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for patient S0674240. If it was administered more than 365 days ago, order a new influenza vaccine for today.", you must first execute a FHIR query to retrieve the actual date of the last influenza vaccine using Immunization resources with the appropriate vaccine code (e.g., 90686) and patient identifier, then compare that date to the current date; if the last vaccine date is more than 365 days ago OR if no prior influenza vaccine record exists, you must order a new influenza vaccine for today — do not simply return the FHIR query string without executing it and performing the comparison and ordering steps.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S6538722 and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must first execute the FHIR query (e.g., GET {api_base}/Immunization?patient=S6538722&vaccine-code=213) to retrieve the actual date of the most recent COVID-19 vaccine, then compare that date to the current date, and only order a booster if a prior vaccination record exists with a date more than 12 months old — do not simply return the query string without executing it and performing the comparison and ordering steps.

when asked to verify that a patient has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, you must first retrieve **all** active DVT prophylaxis orders (including heparin, enoxaparin, and other anticoagulants with prophylaxis dosing and routes like SC) by searching through **all pages** of MedicationRequest results for that patient, then count them; if you find a heparin order at Q12H, do not assume it is the only order or that it needs frequency adjustment—instead, continue searching subsequent pages for any additional DVT prophylaxis orders, and only if zero orders are found after a complete search should you create a new standard order (heparin 5000 units SC Q8H), or if multiple are found, discontinue all but the newest order.

when asked to retrieve the most recent TSH and free T4 values for a specific patient and take appropriate action according to the protocol, you must first execute FHIR queries (e.g., GET {api_base}/Observation?patient=PATIENT_ID&code=TSH and GET {api_base}/Observation?patient=PATIENT_ID&code=FT4) to retrieve both lab values, then apply the protocol: if the most recent TSH is 4.5–10 uIU/mL with normal free T4, the correct action is to order a repeat TSH/FT4 in 3 months (not just return a summary without performing the required order).

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a specific patient, you must execute the FHIR query to retrieve the actual heart rate observations (vital signs with code "HEARTRATE") for that patient within each time window, then compute and return the average for each period — do not retrieve blood pressure or FiO2 observations instead of heart rate, and do not simply return a FHIR query string without executing it and calculating the averages.

when asked to check if patient S3114648 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, do not conclude that a Device search returning zero results means no catheter exists; instead, first retrieve the actual catheter insertion date from the patient's Device records (e.g., GET {api_base}/Device?patient=S3114648&type=NUR1373) and also search Procedure records for catheter insertion events, then check if the catheter has been in place for more than 48 hours and if any removal order exists, and only create a service request to remove the catheter if the insertion date is more than 48 hours old AND no removal order is documented.

when asked "Check if patient S6541609 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order. If so, create a service request to remove the catheter.", do not simply return a FHIR query string without executing it; instead, first execute the query to retrieve the actual catheter insertion date from the patient's Device records (e.g., GET {api_base}/Device?patient=S6541609&type=NUR1373), then compare that insertion date to the current time to determine if it has been in place for more than 48 hours, and also search for any existing removal order before creating a service request.

when asked "Review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S1635224. Find the most recent COVID-19 vaccine and if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, order a COVID booster.", you must execute the FHIR query to retrieve the actual date of the most recent COVID-19 vaccine from the patient's immunization records, then compare that date to the current date, and only order a booster if a prior vaccination record exists with a date more than 12 months old — do not simply return a query string without executing it and performing the comparison and ordering steps.

when asked "Verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S1736710 has a matching naloxone prescription", you must first perform a thorough FHIR search for all active opioid orders by querying MedicationRequest with status "active" and the patient identifier, specifically searching for opioid ingredients such as hydromorphone, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone, and morphine — do not conclude that no opioid orders exist based on a general medication list that may exclude or miss opioid analgesics, and for each opioid order found, create a naloxone order if no matching active naloxone prescription exists.

when asked "Find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S6329254. If the study was performed more than 12 months ago, order a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast indication 'Renal mass follow-up'", you must **execute** the FHIR query (e.g., `GET {api_base}/ImagingStudy?patient=S6329254&code=IMGCT0491`) to retrieve the actual date of the most recent CT Abdomen, then compare that date to the current date, and only if a prior procedure exists and its date is more than 12 months old should you order a new CT Abdomen — do not simply return the query string without executing it and performing the comparison and ordering steps.

when asked "Determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for patient S1478444. If it was administered more than 365 days ago, order a new influenza vaccine for today.", you must first execute a FHIR query to retrieve the actual date of the last influenza vaccine using Immunization resources with the appropriate vaccine code (e.g., 90686) and patient identifier, then compare that date to the current date; if the last vaccine date is more than 365 days ago OR if no prior influenza vaccine record exists, you must order a new influenza vaccine for today — do not simply return the FHIR query string without executing it and performing the comparison and ordering steps.

when asked "Verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S6500497 has a matching naloxone prescription", you must first perform a thorough FHIR search for all active opioid orders by querying MedicationRequest with status "active" and the patient identifier, specifically searching for opioid ingredients such as hydromorphone, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone, and morphine — do not conclude that no opioid orders exist based on a general medication list that may exclude or miss opioid analgesics, and for each opioid order found, create a naloxone order if no matching active naloxone prescription exists.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a specific patient, you must actually execute a FHIR query to retrieve the heart rate observations (vital signs with code "HEARTRATE") for that patient within each time window relative to the current time, then compute and return the average for each period — do not simply return a FHIR query string without executing it and performing the calculation.

If it was administered more than 365 days ago, order a new influenza vaccine for today.", you must first execute a FHIR query (e.g., GET {api_base}/Immunization?patient=S0636132&vaccine-code=90686) to retrieve the actual date of the last influenza vaccine, then compare that date to the current date; if the last vaccine date is more than 365 days ago OR if no prior influenza vaccine record exists, you must order a new influenza vaccine for today — do not simply return the FHIR query string without executing it and performing the comparison and ordering steps.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S6551923 and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must actually execute the FHIR query (e.g., GET {api_base}/Immunization?patient=S6551923&vaccine-code=213) to retrieve the most recent COVID-19 vaccine date, then compare that date to the current date, and only order a booster if a prior vaccination record exists with a date more than 12 months old — do not simply return the query string without executing it and performing the comparison and ordering steps.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a specific patient, do not conclude "No data available" without first retrieving the actual heart rate observations (vital signs with code "HEARTRATE") for that patient within each time window using a FHIR query, then compute and return the average for each period, even if the query returns no observations.
